description: Citations referenced in ChainRight notes, reports, and code. The 'verified' field indicates whether David has personally checked the citation against its primary source. arxiv_id, doi, and url are only populated where the assistant has high confidence; everything else is blank for verification.

citations.authors: Vaswani, A., Shazeer, N., Parmar, N., Uszkoreit, J., Jones, L., Gomez, A. N., Kaiser, L., Polosukhin, I.
citations.title: Attention Is All You Need
citations.notes: The original transformer paper. Cited as the architectural ancestor of GPT-2 and Sonnet.

citations.authors: Kaplan, J., McCandlish, S., Henighan, T., Brown, T. B., Chess, B., Child, R., Gray, S., Radford, A., Wu, J., Amodei, D.
citations.title: Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models
citations.notes: Original scaling-laws paper. Cited for the L = E + A/P^alpha + B/D^beta form.

citations.authors: Hoffmann, J., Borgeaud, S., Mensch, A., et al.
citations.title: Training Compute-Optimal Large Language Models
citations.notes: The compute-optimal P/D ratio ~1:20. Cited for the optimal training proportion result.

citations.title: Validity of the single processor approach to achieving large scale computing capabilities
citations.venue: AFIPS Spring Joint Computer Conference, pp. 483-485
mentioned_in: Conversation: Amdahl's law definition and audit-cost ceiling argument
citations.notes: Original Amdahl's law paper. Cited for S(n) = 1/((1-p) + p/n) and the (1-p) ceiling.

citations.title: Reevaluating Amdahl's Law
mentioned_in: Conversation: weak scaling complement to Amdahl
citations.notes: Weak-scaling counterpart to Amdahl. Cited as the dual perspective on parallel scalability.

citations.authors: Pruthi, G., Liu, F., Sundararajan, M., Kale, S.
citations.title: Estimating Training Data Influence by Tracing Gradient Descent
citations.notes: TracIn / TracIn-CP paper. Cited as the influence-function attribution method used in the H3 rig.

citations.title: Understanding Black-box Predictions via Influence Functions
citations.notes: Foundational influence-function paper. Cited as the precursor to TracIn.

citations.title: Circuit Tracing: Revealing Computational Graphs in Language Models (methods)
citations.notes: Cited in the circuit-tracer README as the methods paper for attribution graphs. URL transcribed from the README.

citations.title: On the Biology of a Large Language Model
citations.notes: Cited in the circuit-tracer README as the biology paper using the attribution method. URL transcribed from the README.

citations.authors: Hu, E. J., Shen, Y., Wallis, P., Allen-Zhu, Z., Li, Y., Wang, S., Wang, L., Chen, W.
citations.title: LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models
citations.notes: LoRA paper. Cited as the parameter-efficient fine-tuning method used in the H3 rig.

citations.authors: Carlini, N., Tramer, F., Wallace, E., et al.
citations.title: Extracting Training Data from Large Language Models
citations.notes: Memorization / training-data extraction. Cited for the claim that membership-inference recovers scraps but not the corpus.

citations.title: GPT-2 Output Dataset
citations.notes: The dataset itself. Cloned and used throughout the project.

citations.authors: Radford, A., Wu, J., Child, R., Luan, D., Amodei, D., Sutskever, I.
citations.title: Language Models are Unsupervised Multitask Learners
mentioned_in: Implicit throughout — gpt-2 dataset is from this work
citations.notes: GPT-2 paper. Cited implicitly via the dataset; should be cited explicitly in the paper's related work.

citations.authors: Wu, S., Irsoy, O., Lu, S., Dabravolski, V., Dredze, M., Gehrmann, S., Kambadur, P., Rosenberg, D., Mann, G.
citations.title: BloombergGPT: A Large Language Model for Finance
citations.notes: BloombergGPT. Cited as evidence that domain-scoped models can match frontier-model performance on finance tasks.

citations.authors: Ilyas, A., Park, S. M., Engstrom, L., Leclerc, G., Madry, A.
citations.title: Datamodels: Predicting Predictions from Training Data
citations.notes: Datamodels paper from the Madry lab. Cited as a frontier-scale-friendly proxy for influence functions.

citations.title: Supervisory Guidance on Model Risk Management (SR 11-7)
citations.notes: Foundational US bank model-risk-management framework. Cited as the regulatory anchor against which AI-deployment audit cost should be measured.

citations.title: Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 — AI Act
citations.venue: Official Journal of the European Union
citations.notes: EU AI Act. Cited for high-risk-system documentation requirements.
